response: Robert M. Fomon was an American financier who served as the Chairman and Chief Executive Officer of E. F. Hutton & Co. from 1970 to 1987, and he was also a governor of the New York Stock Exchange. Additionally, he held the position of Chairman of the Board of Governors of the Pacific Stock Exchange. His connection to the New York Stock Exchange, located at 11 Wall Street, Lower Manhattan, New York City, was through his role as a prominent figure in the financial industry and his involvement with the exchange's governance and operations.
